original: A humpback whale is filmed briefly swallowing a man on a kayak off Chilean Patagonia, before releasing him unharmed
The Great Lego Spill: a rogue wave hits cargo ship Tokio Express off the coast of Cornwall, England, tipping 62 containers into the sea, carrying 4.8m pieces of Lego
The Harvard-Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics discovers the universe's largest known diamond, white dwarf star BPM 37093
6-year-old Texan Stormie Jones gets 1st heart and liver transplant
NASA confirms Mars Opportunity rover's mission has ended after 15 years due to a sandstorm damaging its communications
Space shuttle Discovery captures the Hubble Space Telescope in its cargo bay
Flinders River swells to 37 miles wide (60km) creating its own weather system after intense flooding in Queensland, Australia
Scientists overturn current thought about how planets form - not by violent collision but gentle clumping, through study of Arrokoth in Kepler belt, published in "Science"
Archaeologists announce discovery of oldest known beer factory in Abydos, Egypt, from early Dynastic period 3150 B.C.- 2613 B.C
The last original "Peanuts" comic strip appears in newspapers one day afterCharles M. Schulzdies
Albert Einsteinpresents Karl Schwarzschild’s solution to his equations for a (then theoretical) black hole to the Prussian Academy of Sciences
Wright Flyer, 1st plane to fly, returns to US from England
Moving picture projector patented
Soviet Union fires a rocket from Sputnik V to Venus
Israel acquires 4 of 7 Dead Sea scrolls
Formation of Guardian Angels crime fighters in New York City
"Free Eats" introduces George "Spanky" McFarland to "Our Gang"
Super Bowl LVI halftime show features Hip Hop legendsDr. Dre,Snoop Dogg,Kendrick LamarandMary J. Blige,Eminemand50 Cent
Super Bowl LVI, SoFi Stadium, Inglewood, CA: Los Angeles Rams beat Cincinnati Bengals, 23-20; MVP: Cooper Kupp, LA Rams, WR
Baseball Hall of Famer "Rube" Foster and seven other team owners create the first Negro National League (NNL) at a meeting in a Kansas City YMCA
1st Black pro Basketball team, "Renaissance", organizes
Austrian alpine skier Marcel Hirscher wins the men's combined gold medal at the Pyeongchang Winter Olympics; goes on to also win giant slalom gold
Washington State's Hood Canal Bridge breaks up in windstorm
The LZR Racer Suit is unveiled in NYC; line of competition swimsuits made by Speedo using high-tech fabric of woven elastane-nylon & polyurethane; times went down dramatically; rules changed 2010
English explorersRichard Francis Burtonand John Speke explore Lake Tanganyika in Africa
Tycho Brahefirst sketches "Tychonic system" of solar system
Abraham Lincolndeclared US President in Washington, D.C.
James 'Cool Papa' Bell is named to baseball's Hall of Fame
Future world championRocky Marcianoknocks out Italian heavyweight boxer Gino Buonvino in the 2nd round at Rhode Island Auditorium, Providence for his 40th straight win
Clas Thunbergof Finland wins his 4th Olympic gold medal when he dead-heats with Norway’s Bernt Evensen in the 500m speed skating at the St. Moritz Winter Games
American college basketball player Frank Selvey scores a record 100 points for Furman beating Newberry 149-95
Inna Lassovskaya jumps world record 14.9m
Capitol Records releases the Beatles' double "A" side single "Penny Lane" and "Strawberry Fields" in the US
"Prince Valiant" comic strip appears; known for historical detail
Women's US Marine Corps created
First military action to result in Medal of Honor, as Bernard J.D. Irwin volunteers to lead rescue of 60 soldiers from Chiricahua Indians at Apache Pass, Arizona
Man-eating tiger is reported to have killed 48, 80 km from New Delhi
-16°F (-27°C), Minden Louisiana (state record)
-29°F (-34°C) Pond, Arkansas (state record)
-40°F (-40°C) Warsaw, Missouri (state record)
-40°F (-40°C) Lebanon, Kansas (state record)
-2°F (-19°C) Tallahassee, Florida (state record)
Slopestyle freestyle skiing event makes Olympic debut at Sochi Winter Games; American Joss Christensen is inaugural men's gold medallist
Marte Olsbu Røiseland of Norway wins the women's biathlon pursuit for her third gold medal of the Beijing Winter Olympics (women's sprint & mixed relay)
Merlene Ottey runs world record 200m indoor (21.87 sec)
Swedish cross country skier Gunde Svan wins 15k event, the first of 2 gold medals at the Sarajevo Winter Olympics; also wins 4 x 10k relay gold
Larry Bird(Celtics) ends NBA free throw streak of 71 games
NZ beats West Indies by one wicket in cricket at Dunedin
West Indian batsman Andy Ganteaume scores 112 for W Indies in his only Test Cricket innings
Olympic boxing gold medallist Johnny Douglas takes 5/46 as England cricket team regains Ashes with an innings and 225 run 4th Test win over Australia in Melbourne
January 2020 was the hottest January in recorded history according to National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration
South Africa declares a three-year drought a National Disaster, though pushes Cap Town's "Day Zero" to June 4
NL offices begins move from Cincinnati to San Francisco (completed Feb 23)
Musical "El Grande de Coca-Cola" premieres in NYC
Premier of Tania León's "Stride" for the New York Philharmonic at the David Geffen Hall, Lincoln Center, New York City (2021 Pulitzer Prize for Music)
Soviet steamship SS Chelyuskin sinks in the Arctic Ocean after being crushed by ice packs near Kolyuchin Island in the Chukchi Sea
Work begins on the covering of the Senne, burying Brussels's primary river and creating the modern central boulevards
The feminist newspaper La Citoyenne is first published in Paris by activist Hubertine Auclert
Disfida di Barletta - Famous challenge between 13 Italian and 13 French knights near Barletta, Southern Italy
Battle at Fraustadt: Swedish army beats Russia and Saxon forces, one of Swedish's Greatest victories in the Great Northern War
